exemplar.title: How has Nike's athlete endorsement strategy influenced brand loyalty in the USA sportswear sector from 2015 to 2023?

detailedExemplarData.title: How has Nike's athlete endorsement strategy influenced brand loyalty in the USA sportswear sector from 2015 to 2023?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear identification of âCreativityâ as the key concept with multiple concrete examples
Strong analytical linkage to Nikeâs products, campaigns and segmentation throughout most of the IA
weaknesses: Integration of the key concept becomes intermittent in sections on BCG and sales ratios
Introduction and conclusion lack explicit references back to âCreativityâ for seamless integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work at Band 4 for both understanding and organizational linkage. The student demonstrates a good grasp of creativity and weaves it into the analysis, but occasional lapses and missing explicit ties prevent full integration. A score of 4 reflects strong performance with room for more consistent application.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents provided, meeting the quantitative requirement
Sources are generally relevant, including Nikeâs annual report and reputable secondary articles
weaknesses: Depth of sources is limited, with heavy reliance on secondary materials
Citation formats vary and some data points (e.g., turnover ratios) lack explicit source references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA meets the 3â5 document requirement and uses relevant materials, warranting Band 3. However, limited depth of primary data and inconsistent citations keep it from the highest band, justifying a score of 3 out of 4.

strengths: Adequate selection of multiple tools and theories (BCG, Porter, Aaker, ratio analysis)
Application is generally relevant, with clear examples linking tools to the research question
weaknesses: Toolkit lacks breadth (e.g., missing SWOT or stakeholder analysis)
Some tool discussions drift into description rather than rigorous application
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand feedback places both selection and application solidly in Band 3. The student applies tools effectively but with limited depth and breadth, so a score of 3 reflects an adequate but improvable performance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective use of revenue figures, CAGR and turnover tables to support evaluation
Mostly clear connections drawn between endorsement data and brand loyalty outcomes
weaknesses: Several data points lack precise citation or are approximate without reference
Some data (e.g., Google Trends) is presented without interpretation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis is generally effective (Band 4) with strong evaluation of key datasets and sustained linkage to loyalty. Missing citations and uneven depth prevent Band 5. A score of 4 reflects mostly effective analysis with room for more thorough integration.

strengths: Conclusions restate key findings and explicitly answer the research question
Acknowledges limitations and suggests future research directions
weaknesses: Some claims are asserted without data support
Fails to reinforce the key concept of creativity in the closing remarks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusions are consistent with the evidence and directly address the research question (Band 3 for relevance) but contain unsupported assertions, yielding only partial consistency (Band 2 overall). A score of 2 reflects a need for stronger evidence alignment.

strengths: Logical organization with clear sections (title page, TOC, methodology, analysis, bibliography)
Section headings (e.g., Cash Cows) enhance readability
weaknesses: Minor editing errors (incomplete sentences) interrupt flow
Conclusion could be better structured with subheadings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA exhibits appropriate structure throughout, warranting the maximum Band 2. The minor editing lapses do not detract significantly from the overall logical flow.

strengths: Most required elements (title page, TOC, bibliography) are present
Figures and tables are integrated into the text
weaknesses: Several figures lack captions or source labels, and some bibliography URLs are truncated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission includes many required presentation elements but misses at least one critical item (word count) and has incomplete figure labeling. This aligns with Band 1, yielding a score of 1.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure with title page, TOC, methodology, analysis and bibliography is maintained throughout, enabling reader navigation.

Sources are generally relevant (Nike annual report, Forbes article) yet most are secondary web pieces with limited depth; few provide detailed primary data. Hence relevance is adequate but **depth is thin**.
While most required elements are present, the word-count field is blank and several figures lack captions or source labels, indicating that at least **one required element is missing**.
Exactly five supporting documents are listed, satisfying the 3-5 requirement. Citation formatting errors are present but do not negate the count.

Each tool is applied directly to the research question (e.g., BCG positions endorsed products; turnover ratios linked to endorsements). Relevance is clear, yet discussion sometimes drifts into description (product lists) rather than rigorous application, so effectiveness is **adequate, not exemplary**.

Data sets include revenue, CAGR, turnover ratio and market-share estimates, meeting the criterion for **adequate selection and use**. However, some figures lack citations and precision (e.g., âapprox. 60 % shareâ), preventing higher bands.

The narrative frequently links data to brand-loyalty impacts (e.g., turnover ratio â â loyalty). While evaluation depth varies, the analysis is **mostly effective** with clear connections between endorsements and loyalty outcomes.
The student directly answers the research question in the concluding section, explicitly stating the influence of endorsements on loyalty from 2015-23.

The student selects BCG Matrix, Porterâs Generic Strategies, ratio analysis and Aakerâs Brand Equityâan **adequate** but not especially wide toolkit; stakeholder or SWOT tools that could enrich the topic are absent.

Conclusions restate key findings and are largely aligned with presented evidence, but minor claims (e.g., âlimited-edition products prove retention powerâ) are asserted without data support, so consistency is only **partial**.

**Strengths:** Nike is the sole organisational focus and is woven into every sectionâproduct categories, revenue figures and endorsement deals â demonstrating strong analytical linkage.  
**Limitations:** A few general industry statements (e.g., âsoccer cleats market increased after Messiâ) are not tied back to Nikeâs data, so integration is not fully seamless.

**Strengths:** The student clearly identifies Creativity as the key concept and discusses it in relation to self-lacing shoes, innovative materials and storytelling campaigns. These references show a good grasp of how creativity drives Nikeâs product and marketing decisions.  
**Limitations:** Integration is intermittent; once the discussion moves to BCG and sales ratios, explicit links back to creativity disappear. Hence, the analysis is only **partially integrated**, not âthroughoutâ the IA.

annotations.comment: The student correctly identifies âCreativityâ as the key concept and highlights its relevance to Nikeâs self-lacing shoes and storytelling campaigns, showing strong initial integration.
The word-count field is blank. Include the total word count to satisfy IA requirements and avoid mark penalties.
The table of contents is clear and logically organized, facilitating navigation through the IAâs sections.
The introduction contextualizes Nikeâs history well but does not explicitly link back to âCreativityâ until later. Introduce the key concept here to strengthen integration.
Ansoff Matrix is mentioned in the introduction but omitted from the methodology table. Remove or justify its inclusion to maintain coherence.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The âCash Cowsâ section title clearly delineates this analysis segment, aiding structure and reader orientation.
The Google Trends table is presented but not interpreted. Explain how the trend data supports analysis of endorsement impact.
The year-over-year growth percentages are clear and well presented using inline math.
The suggestion to analyse ROI of endorsement contracts is insightful; consider including a brief ROI calculation or literature reference.
Porterâs Differentiation strategy is well explained, linking exclusive athlete partnerships to premium positioning.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The methodology table omits the ratio analysis tool described in the text. Include it in the table to ensure completeness and clarity.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The BCG matrix figure lacks a caption and source. Add descriptive titles and citations for all figures.
Market-share of âapproximately 60%â is asserted without citation. Provide a precise source or reference for this data point.
Revenue figures for Jordan Brand are precise and well-sourced, supporting robust cash cow analysis.
The self-reported âapprox. 30%â growth for Nike Adapt is unreferenced. Cite a reliable source or adjust the language to reflect data uncertainty.
The discussion of Nikeâs sustainability efforts via Space Hippie is informative but lacks analysis of its effect on brand loyalty.
This table lacks a title and contains empty cells, impairing clarity. Complete data and add headings.
The sales turnover table is detailed and directly linked to endorsements, demonstrating effective data selection.
Include a citation for the sales turnover ratios to confirm data sources and enhance credibility.
The competitor comparison demonstrates thorough analysis of Adidas and Under Armour, enriching the evaluation.
The conclusion acknowledges limitations and future directions, demonstrating balanced evaluation.
The sentence cuts off after âLebronâ suggesting incomplete editing. Complete or revise to maintain coherence.
Segment-specific strategy analysis integrates consumer segmentation well, reinforcing targeted loyalty insights.
Aakerâs Brand Equity Model is applied but could more explicitly tie back to Nikeâs U.S. loyalty metrics.
The conclusion restates findings well but omits explicit linking back to âCreativity.â Reinforce the key concept in closing remarks.
The reference list predominantly uses secondary sources. Incorporate primary data sources (e.g., internal reports) to deepen analysis.
Divide the conclusion into sub-sections or use headings to improve readability and structure.
All five supporting documents are listed, but citation styles vary. Standardize formatting for consistency.
Several URLs in the bibliography are truncated or split across lines. Ensure each link is complete and clickable.
